reasoning: HHS Secretary RFK Jr. formally announced on April 7, 2025, that he is instructing the CDC to stop recommending community water fluoridation, and HHS has reconvened a task force to issue a new recommendation. Additionally, the FDA took immediate action to begin removing prescription fluoride supplements from the market by May 13. Given the explicit direct order from the HHS Secretary to the CDC, the high political prioritization of this issue by RFK Jr., and the fact that altering/removing a CDC webpage is procedurally very simple compared to formal rulemaking, it is highly likely the CDC will pull the existing 0.7 mg/L recommendation page offline or significantly alter it to state it is "under review" before July 1, 2025. Bureaucratic inertia is the only minor chance it remains unchanged.
